title: AWS Config should be enabled and use the service-linked role for resource recording
description: This control checks whether AWS Config is enabled in your account in the current AWS Region, records all resources that correspond to controls that are enabled in the current Region, and uses the service-linked AWS Config role.
what_is_wrong: This control checks whether AWS Config is enabled in your account in the current AWS Region, records all resources that correspond to controls that are enabled in the current Region, and uses the service-linked AWS Config role.
what_the_fix_does: Enables AWS Config recorder and delivery channel for continuous config tracking.

findings.title: AWS Config should be enabled and use the service-linked role for resource recording

recommendation.rationale: Matrix cell risk_medium__criticality_high maps to default recommendation 'pr_only'. Risk is medium and business criticality is high. Criticality evidence: data_sensitivity=0.45, privilege_level=1.00, internet_exposure=0.00, matched_signals=none. Relationship context is incomplete, so the recommendation remains conservative and advisory unless policy overrides it.

execution_guidance.label: Enable AWS Config with account-local delivery
execution_guidance.blast_radius_summary: Affects account-wide security posture for account 696505809372.

pre_checks.message: Confirm this change is intended for AWS::::Account:696505809372 in account 696505809372 (us-east-1).
Confirm the repository, deployment path, and approver that own this infrastructure change before generating the PR bundle.
Enabling AWS Config may increase logging and storage costs.

execution_guidance.expected_outcome: PR mode produces a reviewable infrastructure change: AWS Config should be enabled and use the service-linked role for resource recording After merge and apply through your IaC path, Security Hub should reflect the remediated state within 1-6 hours.

post_checks.message: Review the generated bundle or diff, then capture plan and apply evidence after the change is merged through the normal infrastructure workflow.
Verify the completed change only affected the intended account scope for AWS::::Account:696505809372.
Trigger immediate re-evaluation after the change lands and confirm the action closes without reopening.

rollback.summary: Use this rollback path to revert the infrastructure change through the same reviewed delivery workflow.
notes: Prefer reverting through version control and your normal deployment pipeline so rollback stays auditable.
